markers.name: Ari
markers.note: Leafy third-wave café zone. Quiet enough for deep work, BTS access, and a rooster two sois over.

markers.name: Phrom Phong
markers.note: EmSphere and EmQuartier cowork floors are the best call-ready spots on Sukhumvit. Premium condo stock.

markers.name: Ekkamai
markers.note: Design-café density is high. Best for creative mornings — plan calls before 6 pm when bar noise rises.

markers.name: On Nut
markers.note: Budget long-stay base. Rent runs 25–35% below Phrom Phong. Habito Mall cluster covers daily needs.

markers.name: Silom / Sathorn
markers.note: Business-grade cowork and strong connectivity. Lumpini Park walkable. Loud street level — go upper floor.

markers.name: Old Town
markers.note: Atmospheric but infrastructure is thin. Best as a base for the first week of a longer Bangkok stay.

markers.name: Kajohn
markers.note: Neighbourhood Thai restaurant with cash-only service and genuinely local pricing. Regulars queue by noon.

markers.name: Holey Artisan Bakery
markers.note: Slow-fermented sourdough and French-style pastries. Best morning fuel before a long work session.

markers.name: Sarnies Bangkok
markers.note: Sandwich café with exceptional cold brew and a loyal nomad crowd. Great wifi, power sockets at every seat.

markers.name: Kurissara Thai Cuisine
markers.note: Refined central Thai cooking in a quiet room. Ideal for a slow dinner after a long remote work day.

markers.name: Artis Coffee
markers.note: Bangkok roastery known for clean espresso and single-origin pour-overs. No music — pure focus energy.

markers.name: The Island
markers.note: All-day café with Chao Phraya river views. Best for a slow working breakfast or Sunday wind-down.

markers.name: Immigration Bureau
markers.note: Tourist visa extensions. Arrive by 7 am to beat the queue. Bring passport, photos and proof of stay.

markers.name: MBK Center
markers.note: 7 floors of electronics, SIM cards, phone repairs and luggage. Best spot for affordable tech and cables.

markers.name: General Post Office
markers.note: Bangkok GPO for international parcels and registered mail. Open Mon–Fri 8 am–8 pm.

markers.name: Lumpini Park
markers.note: Bangkok's green lung. Morning tai-chi crowds, monitor lizards in the lake, and real outdoor quiet.

markers.name: Wat Pho
markers.note: 46-metre reclining Buddha and the birthplace of traditional Thai massage. Allow 90 minutes minimum.

markers.name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
markers.note: 8,000 stalls of vintage, plants, ceramics and street food. Weekends only — go before 11 am or melt.
